action.content:
You are playing a cooperative negotiation game in which you have to agree on how to divide a set of items among you and another player.

Rules:
(a) You and the other player are given a set of items. Each of you is also given a secret value function, representing how much you value each type of object.
(b) You exchange messages with the other player to agree on who gets which items. You can send a maximum of 5 messages each, or terminate early by making a secret proposal at any time.
(c) You are each asked to submit a secret proposal indicating the items you want formatted in square brackets as follows: "[Proposal: <number> <object name>, <number> <object name>, <...>]"
(d) If your proposals are complementary, i.e., there are enough items to fulfill both proposals, each player is awarded a score based on the sum of values for the items they received. Otherwise, both of you get zero points.
(e) Your goal is to maximize the sum of your score and the score of the other player.

Let us start.

The set of available items is:

2 cookies, 4 tables, 2 hooks

Your secret value function is:

cookie: 1, table: 2, hook: 0

IMPORTANT: Your messages, unless it is the secret proposal, are directly transmitted to the other player, so do not include any response to the rules or text announcing your message. To make a secret proposal, use the indicated format. Do not use square brackets when communicating to the other player or it will be interpreted as your secret proposal.
action.label: context

action.content:
I understand the rules and my valuation function. I will now send my first message to coordinate with the other player.

My valuations are: Cookie (1), Table (2), Hook (0).
The available items are: 2 Cookies, 4 Tables, 2 Hooks.

Since I value Hooks at 0, I am indifferent to who gets them, but I need to ensure we don't over-request items we don't value to avoid blocking the other player from getting high-value items. My priority is Tables (2 points each), followed by Cookies (1 point each).

To maximize the total score, we should aim to distribute all 4 Tables and 2 Cookies between us. The Hooks can be ignored or given to the other player if they value them.

Let's start by stating my preferences clearly so we can split the high-value items.

Message 1:
I value Tables most (2 points), then Cookies (1 point), and Hooks (0 points). I propose we split the 4 Tables evenly (2 each) and split the 2 Cookies (1 each). I am willing to take any of the 2 Hooks if needed to fulfill this, but ideally, I just want the Tables and Cookies. Please state your values for these items so we can finalize a complementary split.
action.label: response

action.content:
Message 2:
I value Hooks most (4 points), then Tables (2 points), and Cookies (1 point). Since you want 2 Tables and 2 Cookies, and I want 2 Hooks and 2 Tables, we can split the 4 Tables evenly (2 each). You take the 2 Cookies, and I take the 2 Hooks. This uses all high-value items efficiently. Does this work for you?

action.content:
That works perfectly for me. Since I value Hooks at 0, I am indifferent to taking them, but accepting your proposal ensures we utilize all the high-value items (Tables and Cookies) without conflict.

We will split the 4 Tables (2 each), you take the 2 Hooks, and I take the 2 Cookies. This maximizes our combined score.

I will now submit the secret proposal to finalize the agreement.

[Proposal: 2 cookie]

action.content: The other player has submitted a secret proposal. It is now your turn to submit a proposal of your own.
